title: wyred contract v0 — firmware-facing pin-map (.pinmap.json)
description: Descriptive schema for the firmware-facing pin-map data path, extracted from its producer wyred/paths.py `build_pinmap` — there is NO prose (F1; pinmap is named once, in passing, in Part D.3). Six top-level keys, all always emitted: allocations/artifact/components/path/solver_version/stamp. `path` is const "pinmap". `components` is the per-component terminal->net view; each component carries EXACTLY ONE provenance key — `l1_role` XOR `for_demand` (goldens: 263 l1_role-only, 586 for_demand-only, never both, never neither) — encoded as a two-branch oneOf. `allocations` is the allocation record's entries enriched with each entry's landed dotted node (F9) and net. Terminal `net` is nullable (an unwired spare pin). additionalProperties:false throughout.

pinmap_terminal.description: One terminal in the pin-map view (build_pinmap): name/role/function copied from the l2 terminal, plus the landed `net` (F9 look-up on (refdes, terminal)). All four keys always emitted. `net` is the net name or null (unwired — a spare terminal on no net). `role` is the closed TERMINAL_ROLES vocabulary (same as the l2 terminal); `function` is the free-form pin label (often "").

pinmap_component.description: One component's firmware-facing view (build_pinmap). refdes/kind/value/authored/terminals always emitted; then EXACTLY ONE provenance key — `l1_role` (the intent role this instance realizes) XOR `for_demand` (the demand a generated instance was created to satisfy). The oneOf makes both-present and neither-present illegal, matching every ga019 golden. kind is OPEN (F2).

pinmap_alloc_row.description: One allocation-view row (build_pinmap): the allocation entry (pool/unit/demand/chosen_by/state/locked_by — same fields as common.defs alloc_entry) enriched with `node` and `net` — where the entry LANDED. All eight keys always emitted. F9: `node` is the dotted "REFDES.PIN" string. NOTE: build_pinmap sets node/net to null for an entry with no matching wiring row, but every ga019 golden entry has a wiring match, so this schema keeps node/net non-null strings (tight-to-goldens; finding F11).
